issues.title: Playtest v0.11.0: defeat-pacing drag + hiring pipeline feels gappy (coalesce/de-branch)
issues.body:
From Pip's live v0.11.0 playtest (2026-07-18). Post-alpha design work; NOT release-blocking (the end-game quit is separate and release-blocking -- see branch fix/endgame-quit-score-post).

## 1. Defeat pacing is a drag
Getting defeated took too long / felt low-stakes. Complements the existing "no loss in first ~6 turns" heuristic: the difficulty curve needs a genuine FAILURE RAMP after the safe early window, so the mid/late game has escalating stakes instead of a slow grind to the end. Tune the doom/finance/reputation pressure so a losing run resolves in a satisfying arc, not a war of attrition.

## 2. Hire -> onboard -> equip -> mentor pipeline feels incomplete
The pipeline doesn't read as fully there yet -- "a few little gaps in logics", and the onboard/equip/mentor stages don't land clearly. Pip's framing: "more coalescing and de-branching needs to happen" -- consolidate overlapping/parallel paths, and cut or finish half-wired branches so the flow is one coherent legible sequence rather than several partial ones.

Overall dev read: "internally about where I expect it to be" + "great progress" -- these are refinement items, not a teardown.

issues.title: Local Leaderboard.add_score does not dedupe by entry_uuid (remote PHP does)
issues.body:
Surfaced by the property tests in #699 (`test_property_reentry_rank_stable`).

## Finding
`godot/scripts/leaderboard.gd::add_score` unconditionally `entries.append(entry)` -- there is **no `entry_uuid` dedup**. The only `entry_uuid` logic is the rank-lookup loop. So:
- Adding the same `entry_uuid` twice **appends a duplicate** (board grows until the cap trims it).
- At cap (50), a duplicate `entry_uuid` can occupy two slots and **evict a distinct legitimate entry**.

## Severity: LOW (local-only)
- The **remote** PHP endpoint (`server/leaderboard/score_api.php`) **does** dedupe by `entry_uuid` (verified live: a re-POST returns `duplicate:true`, no double-count). So the shared board friends see is safe.
- This affects only the **local in-game** leaderboard display, and only if the same `entry_uuid` is added twice (normally each game-over generates a fresh uuid, so it needs a double-add/retry path to trigger).

## Fix
Dedupe by `entry_uuid` in `add_score` (skip or replace when the uuid already exists), matching the remote behaviour and the field's implied uniqueness. Add a property test asserting size-stability on re-add once fixed.

Not release-blocking for the v0.11.0 friends & family alpha (remote board is correct); good fast-follow.

issues.title: art: sweep review 2026-07-17 (verdicts + rulings) + tabbed viewer
issues.body: ## What Persists Pip's 2026-07-17 pixel-art review (sweep + re-roll batches) as the durable source of truth, adds Claude's design rulings, and rebuilds the art-review viewer. ### Docs - **`docs/art/reviews/SWEEP_REVIEW_2026-07-17.md`** (new): Pip's verdicts VERBATIM under `## Keep` / `## Maybe` / `## Re-roll` / `## Other notes`, plus `## Style direction` + `## Winning styles`. Then `## Rulings (Claude)` (unifying principle "Doom is a layer, not a repaint"; style lock; 7 rulings on Pip's tagged questions) and `## Re-roll work queue` (HELD pending Pip go), grouped by corrected approach. - **`docs/art/PALETTE_AND_DOOM_INTENSITY.md`**: adds the "Doom is a layer, not a repaint" core principle + a stub operational doom ladder table (per band: glow hex / glow amount / ambient grade / distortion). ### Tooling - **`tools/art_review/verdicts.json`**: seeds 255 verdicts (99 keep / 57 maybe / 99 re-roll) + 171 notes, mapped onto viewer cell ids. Every id verified against a real cell (0 unmatched, 0 skipped). Existing style-matrix picks + notes preserved. - **`tools/art_review/build.py`**: rebuilds `style_review.html` into a single-file **tabbed** viewer: - **Group-by toggle** `Style | Category` -- STYLE is primary (Pip iterates style over stable prompts); auto-falls back to Category when the dataset has only one style value. - **Status filter** `All / Pending / Keep / Maybe / Re-roll`, orthogonal, applies within the active tab. - **Lazy render**: only the active tab's cells hit the DOM. - **Relative-path ``** instead of base64 -> **144 KB** (was ~1.66 MB); scales to bigger sweeps. - Single shared `localStorage` + `verdicts.json` store (one HTML file). Keyboard nav (arrows, K/M/R, N/Enter, Esc) + markdown export preserved. `style_review.html` is regenerable (`python tools/art_review/build.py`) and intentionally NOT committed. ## Verify - `python tools/art_review/build.py` -> `cells=297 missing_img=0`, 143,914 bytes. - HTML parses; group/filter/tab markup present; embedded DATA + SEEDED JSON valid. - 4 files staged by explicit path; no `.import`/`.uid`/`style_review.html` churn. ## Note / regression The old per-matrix "winner radio" UI is gone (it was tied to the endless-scroll matrix layout); `picks` still round-trips through the store + export and the style TABS now serve the compare-styles purpose. Flag if you want winner-picking re-added as a per-style-tab control. Do NOT merge -- for Pip's review. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: feat(audio): adaptive doom-band music -- spec + working prototype + catalogue + analysis template
issues.body:
Executes `docs/audio/MUSIC_BRIEF.md` -- all four deliverables, one focused pass. Do not merge without Pip review.

## 1. Music-design spec (`docs/audio/MUSIC_DESIGN.md`)
Music as the AUDIO expression of `docs/art/PALETTE_AND_DOOM_INTENSITY.md`:
- **Catastrophe axis -> rhythm** (percussive density; FIRE layer at high tiers only)
- **Weirdness axis -> timbre** (clean -> detuned/glitchy -> dissonant/eldritch)
- **Doom bands -> live layers**: 7 canonical `ThemeManager` bands collapse to 5 music tiers named after the art spec's bands: `cosy / uneasy / spooky / eldritch / terminal` (`MUSIC_TIER_BY_BAND = [0,1,1,2,2,3,4]`). No thresholds duplicated -- music reads the canonical band API, so retuning bands retunes music.
- Stem model (BASE / PULSE / WEIRD / FIRE per tier), transition rules (prompt up-moves, lazy down-moves), and the single slot-in point for composed stems.

## 2. Working prototype (`godot/autoload/music_manager.gd`)
GAMEPLAY context now plays a native **`AudioStreamInteractive`**: one looping clip per tier, any-to-any crossfade transition (8 beats @ nominal 120 BPM = 4 s); the `terminal` tier layers two tracks through **`AudioStreamSynchronized`** to keep the vertical-stem path exercised. Existing owned tracks are the PLACEHOLDER tier stems.

**Read-only doom plumbing (ADR-0006 safe):** `GameManager.game_state_updated` (existing broadcast) -> reads `state["doom"]` -> `ThemeManager.get_doom_band_index` -> tier -> `switch_to_clip`. MusicManager only LISTENS; zero changes to game/core code -- the diff touches only the autoload, tests, and docs.

**Degradation:** missing stem -> skipped; empty tier -> inherits neighbour; nothing loads -> legacy playlist; no assets at all -> silence. Never blocks or crashes.

**Hear it:** run the game, start a session -- gameplay opens on `Descent gradient` (cosy). As doom crosses 15% the music crossfades to `Local maxima` (uneasy), 52% -> `Power spike` (spooky), 80% -> `Undetected sandbagging` (eldritch), 92% -> sandbagging + spike layered (terminal cacophony). Doom relief slides it back down. `MusicManager.debug_force_tier(n)` forces a tier for manual testing.

## 3. Stem/track catalogue (`docs/audio/STEM_CATALOGUE.md`)
All 7 tracks (puns preserved, lengths measured from imported streams) + all 7 root-`sounds/` SFX, with vibe/rights/adaptive-slot/coherence notes. Notable finds: `seleciton beeyoowee.wav` is a 0.72 s UI blip sitting in the menu MUSIC playlist; VICTORY playlist is empty (win screen is silent); root `sounds/` SFX provenance is undocumented -- flagged verify-before-ship.

## 4. Track-analysis template (`docs/audio/TRACK_ANALYSIS.md`)
Five-pass listening method (structure map, tempo, key/harmony, voices, production+grip) written for a low-music-literacy owner, a findings->commission-direction translation table, and the fill-in template. No track invented -- Pip supplies it later.

## Honest placeholder-vs-real ledger
- REAL: the adaptive plumbing, doom-band wiring, degradation paths, tier mapping, docs.
- PLACEHOLDER: the audio itself -- full ambient tracks standing in for per-tier stems; no shared tempo grid, so transitions crossfade on a nominal BPM rather than beat-locking. Composed stems slot into `MUSIC_TIER_STEMS` as a data edit.
- OUT OF SCOPE (per brief): composing/generating finished music; AI music gen; bus-layout changes.

## Verification
- Fast gate: `python scripts/run_godot_tests.py --quick --ci-mode --min-tests 300` -> **421 tests, 0 failures, 45/45 files** (includes new `test_adaptive_music.gd`: mapping, monotonic ratchet, config-shape, headless end-to-end play + tier switch, listen-only wiring).
- Simulation/determinism tier: `python scripts/run_godot_tests.py --simulation` -> **93 tests, 0 failures** (audio touched nothing deterministic).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: feat(ui): OfficeFloor directional walk cycles (E/N/W) -- real per-direction walk anims
issues.body:
## What

Adds real per-direction walk animations for the OfficeFloor worker, replacing the static rotation-frame fallback that #662 left in place.

When a worker walks **left / up / down**, they now play a proper walk cycle facing that way, instead of a frozen facing frame. South keeps using the base `walking` clip.

This is the polish that finishes Pip's #662 feedback ("characters should turn and face the direction they walk").

## How

- `employee_sprite.gd::_update_facing_visual()` already prefers a `walking_<facing>` clip when it exists (the seam deliberately left in #662). This PR just supplies those clips -- **zero code change**, pure art + resource wiring.
- 18 new frames (6 each for east / north / west), pixellab.ai char `0a6a27ed` (ArtLoop base-128, locked style), `walking-6-frames` template per direction.
- `office_worker.tres` grows from 4 -> 7 clips (`walking_east`, `walking_north`, `walking_west` added at speed 8.0, looping, matching the base south walk).

## Test

- `test_office_floor_sprite_frames.gd` updated to guard the grown resource (7 clips; each directional walk = 6 frames, all loop, all textures valid).
- Fast gate green: **414 tests, 0 failures**.

## Review

Run the demo and watch a worker path left/up/down:
```
godot --path godot res://scenes/ui/office_floor/office_floor_demo.tscn
```

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: docs: player-facing factual fixes + voice-rewrite teardown for ship (Pip review)
issues.body:
Ship-hygiene pass over **player-facing** docs (README top priority). Two kinds of change, kept deliberately separate.

## The core truth (verified in code)
`game_state.gd check_win_lose()` (L518-523) only ever sets `victory=false`, on `doom>=100` or `reputation<=0`. There is **no `doom<=0` victory branch** and `victory` is never set `true` anywhere. **The game is unwinnable by design** (ADR-0002 / DQ-1 victory-removal, RESOLVED). All fixes below use the true thesis: **you can't win — you can only buy time; survival duration is the score; every run ends in loss; a good run beats your own baseline.** (An earlier revision of this PR wrongly described a doom→0 "apex victory," following stale prose in the ADR-0002 *document* — that has been purged everywhere; see the audit report's correction note.)

## Factual fixes — safe to merge
- **README** — removed the advertised "Survive 100 turns with P(Doom) at 0%" win condition AND the About-para "…whether P(Doom) reaches 0% or humanity faces extinction" win-lie → buy-time framing. Fixed dead `docs/CONTRIBUTORS.md` link.
- **STEAM_INTEGRATION_ROADMAP** — version `v0.10.5 → v0.11.0` (×2); purged both win-lies in the store copy (short description + gameplay bullet).
- **PLAYERGUIDE** — corrected false "Python/Pygame version" note; dropped dead `CONFIG_SYSTEM.md` link.
- **docs/README** (index) — removed two all-dead link sections (8 dead links) + literal `CLIPBOARD`/`MICROSCOPE` emoji-token garbage + dead Dev Tools link.
- **CONTRIBUTING** — removed dead `docs/CONTRIBUTORS.md` link.
- **QUICK_REFERENCE** — version `v0.4.1 → v0.11.0`.

12 factual fixes total. No `.import` churn; `settings.local.json` excluded.

## Voice teardowns — **your worklist, not done here**
`docs/qa/PLAYER_FACING_AUDIT_2026-07-16.md` is the primary deliverable: verdict table + per-doc skeletons (README pitch, PLAYERGUIDE full rewrite — it documents the *pygame-era* game, DEMO_GUIDE archive, Steam store copy, PRIVACY, CHANGELOG L1-wave entries).

## Findings for other lanes (NOT fixed here)
- **In-game HUD center string** `"Win: P(Doom) = 0% or beat baseline | Lose: P(Doom) = 100%"` is the same stale win-lie, live in the game — owned by the HUD/code lane.
- **ADR-0002 doc prose is itself stale** (claims a `doom<=0` code victory that doesn't exist) — reconcile to DQ-1.
- **Steam achievement table** ("First Victory: Win your first game", "Safety Champion: Win with 0% doom", …) is win-based and invalid for an unwinnable game — must be redesigned around survival milestones by the achievements lane (also references deleted legacy python).

**Note:** `AP → Attention` wording was deliberately **flagged, not rewritten** — AP still co-exists in the shipped build (L2/#613 not built yet).

**Please don't merge blind:** the factual half is safe; the teardown half is for you.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: docs: visual asset options menu + pipeline inventory (Pip pick-and-iterate)
issues.body:
## What

A **menu of dials** for visual assets so Pip can pick a direction and iterate fast in parallel with playtesting — plus a full pipeline + asset inventory. **Research/prep lane: doc only. No images generated, no keys used, no binary assets committed.** Do NOT merge — this is for Pip to pick from.

New file: `docs/art/VISUAL_ASSET_OPTIONS_2026-07-16.md`

## Key findings

- **Pipeline runs today.** `tools/assets/generate_images.py` (gpt-image-1.5 default; dormant Gemini "Nano Banana Pro" backend with reference-image consistency). Deps installed locally (openai 2.8.0, Pillow 12.0.0, pyyaml); dry-run verified. **Only gap: `OPENAI_API_KEY` unset**, and the default model id carries a `TODO: confirm exact id vs live docs` — do one throwaway image to confirm id + true cost before a paid batch.
- **Existing scoped ideas found (refresh, not reinvent):** `art_prompts/hero_banners.yaml` (3 scene banners, already on-register, still `pending`); `docs/assets/ASSET_GENERATION_BATCH.md` (126-asset list, but **stale cyan/blue palette** — needs refresh); `ICON_MAPPING_AUDIT.md` (8 icons still rendering as placeholders).
- **Top gaps ranked:** (1) researcher/staff portraits — pure text today, the XCOM/attachment lever; (2) event/response-window hero art; (3) title/menu hero + CRT frame; (4) 8 placeholder-killer icons; (5) doom-meter (mostly wire-up of existing unused art); (6) office background; (7) ledger/finance chrome (deprioritise — deadpan text is on-tone).
- **Recommended first batch (~$2.85, ~51 imgs):** 3 scoped hero banners as-is + 8 refreshed placeholder icons + 6 researcher-portrait pilot.

## Open decisions for Pip
Portrait style (flat CRT-dossier vs painterly XCOM) · matched cast (Gemini+ref) vs independent · confirm model id/cost · per-genre event banners now or later · CRT frame worth mocking. See §7.

issues.title: Check Godot port for team_player uncapped-stacking exploit + poaching rate (salvaged from pre-Godot notes)
issues.body:
**Salvaged from the pre-Godot PERSONNEL_BALANCING_NOTES.md during the 2026-07-16 archival sweep.** Two personnel-balance concerns from the pygame era — verify whether they survived into the Godot port, fix if present. For the QA/bug-shakedown phase, not a blocker.

1. **team_player uncapped-stacking (potential exploit).** Old notes flagged a team_player trait whose synergy bonus stacked without a cap — N team-players compounding into a runaway surplus. By our exploit definition (engine misbehaviour, not clever strategy — DESIGN_PHILOSOPHY), an uncapped stack is a bug to get ahead of. Action: grep Godot researcher/trait code for additive-per-teammate synergy without a cap; if present, cap it + add a test. If traits are not ported yet, note so it is designed-out (ties DQ-15).

2. **Poaching rate.** Old notes had rival poaching ~4%/turn; Pip's target is roughly once a year (ADR-0011 / DQ-22 aggressive-hiring). Reconcile the current cadence to the once-a-year intuition (re-denominate like the other per-tick artifacts calibration caught).

Both feed DQ-15 (researcher archetypes) and DQ-22 (world-shoots-back midgame).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Add visual highlight effect for newly available actions
issues.body:
## Summary
When new actions become available (unlocked by meeting requirements), they should have a visual highlight effect until the player interacts with them.

## Proposed Implementation
- Track which actions player has 'seen' or interacted with
- New/unseen actions get a subtle glow or pulsing border
- Effect disappears after:
  - Player hovers over the action, OR
  - Player uses the action, OR
  - Next turn starts (optional)

## Example Use Cases
- Player publishes first paper → 'Submit Paper' action unlocks → highlighted
- Player reaches 30 reputation → 'Strategic' submenu unlocks → highlighted
- New hire types become available after certain turn → highlighted

## Technical Considerations
- Need to track 'seen' actions in GameState or separate tracking
- Could use shader effect or animated border
- Should be subtle, not distracting

## Priority
Nice-to-have - helps player discovery of new options

issues.title: Add hero art/images to event popup options
issues.body:
## Summary
Event popup buttons should have small 'hero art' images to make choices more visually appealing and help players quickly understand options.

## Proposed Implementation
- Add optional 'icon' field to event option definitions
- Display small art/icon next to each choice button
- Could use existing icon system or add event-specific art

## Example
For burnout event:
- Emergency Intervention: image of manager talking with team
- Team Retreat: image of outdoor bonding activity  
- Pizza Party: image of pizza boxes 🍕
- Push Through: image of stressed workers

## Priority
Nice-to-have, not blocking. Improves visual polish and player experience.

issues.title: Research Quality System (Rushed/Standard/Thorough)
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement research quality toggle that affects research speed, technical debt, and doom.

**Extracted from**: #429 (which covered Tech Debt + Upgrades + Research Quality)

---

## Feature Description

Three quality levels for research actions:

### Rushed
- **Speed**: 2x research generation
- **Cost**: Accumulates technical debt (+2 per turn)
- **Doom**: +1 doom per turn
- **Use Case**: Desperate situations, final push

### Standard
- **Speed**: 1x research generation (baseline)
- **Cost**: No debt
- **Doom**: Baseline doom
- **Use Case**: Normal gameplay

### Thorough
- **Speed**: 0.5x research generation
- **Cost**: No debt
- **Doom**: -1 doom per turn (safer!)
- **Use Case**: Safety-focused builds, early game

---

## Implementation

### Code Changes

```gdscript
# In actions.gd or new research_quality.gd:
const RESEARCH_QUALITY = {
    "rushed": {
        "research_multiplier": 2.0,
        "debt_per_turn": 2,
        "doom_modifier": 1.0
    },
    "standard": {
        "research_multiplier": 1.0,
        "debt_per_turn": 0,
        "doom_modifier": 0.0
    },
    "thorough": {
        "research_multiplier": 0.5,
        "debt_per_turn": 0,
        "doom_modifier": -1.0
    }
}
```

### Files to Modify
- `godot/scripts/core/actions.gd` - Add quality parameter to research actions
- `godot/scripts/core/game_state.gd` - Track current research quality setting
- `godot/scripts/core/turn_manager.gd` - Apply quality modifiers during turn processing
- `godot/scenes/main.tscn` or UI - Add quality selector (radio buttons/dropdown)

### Integration Points
- Technical Debt System (already implemented) - rushed research adds debt
- Doom System (already implemented) - quality affects doom modifiers

---

## UI Requirements
- Radio buttons or dropdown on research actions
- Show trade-offs clearly (speed vs safety vs debt)
- Default: Standard
- Remember last selection

---

## Estimated Effort
1-2 hours implementation + 1 hour testing

---

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Players can select research quality level
- [ ] Research speed scales with quality selection
- [ ] Rushed research accumulates technical debt
- [ ] Thorough research reduces doom
- [ ] UI clearly shows trade-offs

issues.title: Comprehensive upgrades system overhaul
issues.body:
## Summary
The 'upgrades' section needs a comprehensive overhaul tied into useful office and operational expenses.

## Context
From playtesting session 2025-11-27. Current upgrades feel disconnected from actual gameplay needs.

## Proposed Categories
### Office & Facilities
- Workspace quality (desks, chairs, lighting)
- Kitchen and amenities
- Meeting rooms
- Security systems
- Location upgrade (move to better office)

### Equipment & Infrastructure
- Compute clusters
- Development tools
- Network infrastructure
- Backup systems

### Software & Systems
- Accounting software (see #475)
- Project management tools
- Communication platforms
- HR systems

### Research Tools
- Datasets
- Specialized hardware
- Cloud credits
- Academic subscriptions

## Mechanics
- Each upgrade has:
  - Upfront cost
  - Ongoing maintenance
  - Tangible gameplay benefit
  - Clear prerequisite chain

## Integration
- Tied to budget management
- Visible in office representation
- Affects researcher productivity/morale
- Strategic choices: what to prioritize?

## Related Systems
- Office outfitting (#467)
- Finance management (#475)
- Personnel system

## Priority
High - core gameplay loop improvement

issues.title: Progressive finance and software management system
issues.body:
## Summary
Comprehensive finance management system where players upgrade from pen-and-paper to sophisticated software, with increasing complexity and automation options.

## Context
From playtesting session 2025-11-27 - extensive notes on finance system design.

## Core Concept
Players must manage increasing transaction volume and complexity. Better systems cost money but save time (AP). Creates interesting trade-off between efficiency and cost.

## Progression Tiers
### Tier 1: Manual (Free, High AP Cost)
- Pen and paper ledger
- Manual receipt tracking
- Scientists often forget receipts
- Manual payroll calculation
- High AP cost for basic accounting

### Tier 2: Spreadsheet ($100/year, Medium AP Cost)
- Excel/Sheets
- Formulas reduce errors
- Still manual data entry
- Better visibility
- Medium AP cost

### Tier 3: Basic Accounting ($1000/year, Low AP Cost)
- QuickBooks equivalent
- Automated categorization
- Invoice generation
- Still requires manual review
- Low AP cost

### Tier 4: Automated Payroll ($5000/year + per employee)
- Automatic payroll processing
- Tax calculations
- Direct deposit
- Minimal AP cost

### Tier 5: International Payroll ($15000/year + per employee)
- Multi-currency support
- International tax compliance
- Exchange rate handling
- Very low AP cost

### Tier 6: Enterprise ERP ($50000/year)
- Full automation
- Predictive budgeting
- Fraud detection
- Minimal AP cost, maximum functionality

## Required Initial Setup
Players must establish (or have in starting config):
- **Bank account** - where money lives
- **Email** - for communications
- **Tracking system** - how they know their balance (even if just mental math)

## Finance Actions & AP Costs
### Review Transactions (AP varies by system)
- Tier 1: 2 AP per 10 transactions
- Tier 6: 0 AP, auto-approved unless flagged

### Chase Missing Receipts (AP varies by culture)
- Some researchers forget receipts
- Requires follow-up
- Better culture = fewer missing receipts

### Approve Expenses (AP varies by system)
- Tier 1: Manual calculation and approval (high AP)
- Tier 6: Auto-approval with smart rules (0 AP)

### Payroll Processing (AP varies by system)
- Tier 1: Calculate hours, taxes, write checks (5 AP)
- Tier 4+: Automated (0 AP)

## Budget Management Features
### Project Budgets
- Researchers propose projects with budgets
  - Compute hours needed
  - Time estimate
  - Effort required
  - Travel for conferences
- Player approves/modifies budget
- Track spending vs budget
- Overspending warnings

### Recurring Expenses
- Rent, utilities, subscriptions
- Players must anticipate and plan
- Can doom yourself with commitments
- Example: Employee severance, lease agreements

### Commitments & Consequences
- Firing employees requires severance pay
- Breaking lease = penalty
- Subscription contracts
- Loan repayment schedules

## UI Enhancement Tie-In
Each software upgrade also enhances the game's UI:
- Tier 1: Basic text display
- Tier 3: Charts and graphs
- Tier 6: Beautiful dashboards, predictive analytics

## Complexity Scaling
As lab grows, transaction volume increases:
- 1-5 employees: ~50 transactions/month
- 5-20 employees: ~200 transactions/month  
- 20+ employees: ~500+ transactions/month

Without upgrades, this becomes unmanageable.

## Strategic Depth
### Trade-offs
- Hire admin staff OR buy better software?
- Cheap manual processes OR expensive automation?
- Focus budget on research OR infrastructure?

### Failure States
- Overdraft from missed payments
- Tax penalties from errors
- Researcher frustration from slow reimbursement
- Audit problems from poor records

## Implementation Phases
1. **Phase 1**: Basic transaction tracking and manual system
2. **Phase 2**: Software upgrade tiers 2-4
3. **Phase 3**: Project budgeting system
4. **Phase 4**: Advanced automation and ERP
5. **Phase 5**: UI enhancement tie-ins

## Related Systems
- Upgrades overhaul (#475)
- Project/research system (needs creation)
- Employee management
- Budget planning

## Design Principles
- Don't simulate actual accounting (boring)
- Represent complexity abstractly (transactions/month, AP cost)
- Make upgrades feel worth it
- Create genuine strategic choices
- Reward planning and investment in infrastructure

## Priority
High - core economic gameplay loop

## Notes
This is a major system that touches many parts of the game. Should be designed carefully and implemented incrementally.

issues.title: Automated Blog Publishing From Data Lake Events & Commits
issues.body:
This issue tracks design and implementation of an automated blog publishing system for the AI safety research game ecosystem, leveraging code and workflow elements from both pdoom-data and pdoom1 repositories.

**Goals:**
- Automate blog content creation from commit metadata, dataset refresh logs, delta event updates, and pipeline release notes (see docs/guides/PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md, scripts/blog_manager.sh in pdoom-data)
- Make blog generation repeatable, friendly to versioning, and tightly integrated with automated data pipelines
- Ensure each blog entry is traceable, with actionable links to relevant dataset commits, historical events, and game state delta updates
- Maintain world-best documentation and metadata for blog entries, with source attribution and validation, following contributing guidelines
- Automate publication to dev-blog/entries/ and DEVBLOG.md, compatible with CI and future Jira/GitHub automation
- Support cross-repo triggers, allowing event publication from both game (pdoom1) and data (pdoom-data) sides

**References to Implementation**:

- [scripts/blog_manager.sh](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom-data/blob/main/scripts/blog_manager.sh): Bash script for generating and publishing blog post files
- [DEVBLOG.md](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom-data/blob/main/DEVBLOG.md): Main developer blog document
- [docs/guides/PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom1/blob/main/docs/guides/PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md): Automated sync and publishing pipeline, PR automation, documentation standards
- [archive/session-docs/SESSION_SUMMARY_PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_2025-11-03.md](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom1/blob/main/archive/session-docs/SESSION_SUMMARY_PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_2025-11-03.md): Data flow, zero manual intervention, world-best pipeline
- [docs/DATA_PUBLISHING_STRATEGY.md](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom-data/blob/main/docs/DATA_PUBLISHING_STRATEGY.md): Maintenance, update cadence, changelog management

---

**Relevant Code/Workflow Snippets:**

1. *Blog generation & publishing pipeline (pdoom-data)*:
   - [`scripts/blog_manager.sh` (function: publish_blog_post, post creation, validation, permanent release note storage)](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom-data/blob/main/scripts/blog_manager.sh#L73-L290)
   - [`DEVBLOG.md` and [`blog/`](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom-data/tree/main/blog/) structure (timestamped blog files, development log aggregation)]
   - [Pipeline: commit → validation script → dev_metrics.db → DEVBLOG.md update]
   
2. *Sync pipeline from data repo to game repo (pdoom-data → pdoom1)*:
   - [Automated sync pipeline definition, validation, and transformation](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom1/blob/main/docs/guides/PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md#L10-L401)
   - [Sync script for game integration: `scripts/sync_to_pdoom1.sh`](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom1/blob/main/scripts/sync_to_pdoom1.sh)
   - [GitHub Actions automation for data-to-game sync and test passes](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom1/blob/main/docs/guides/PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md#L404-L435)
   
3. *Game event processing and blog traceability*:
   - [session summaries for integration architecture](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom1/blob/main/archive/session-docs/SESSION_SUMMARY_PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_2025-11-03.md#L164-L194)
   - [Database schema for blog entries, cross-repo linkage, and analytics](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom-data/blob/main/docs/INTEGRATION_PLAN.md#L118-L143)

---

**Action Items:**
- [ ] Design blog automation pipeline (event triggers, update hooks)
- [ ] Refactor code/scripts where needed to support reproducible publishing
- [ ] Ensure correct/complete metadata and provenance tracking for all published entries
- [ ] Implement and test automated publishing via CI/CD
- [ ] Document how to contribute content/events to the blog and trace its source

**Success criteria:**
- 100% blog posts generated with complete provenance and metadata
- Automated integration with game, website, and data pipelines
- Zero manual copy-paste required for publishing

---

**Expert Reference Code Snippets:**

*Blog post generation and publishing (pdoom-data):*
```bash
# In scripts/blog_manager.sh
create_blog_post() {
    # ...
    echo "Created blog post: $blog_file"
    echo "Edit the file to add your content, then run:"
    echo "  $0 publish $blog_file"
}
publish_blog_post() {
    local blog_file="$1"
    # ...
    echo "Blog file not found: $blog_file"
    exit 1
}
# ... Functionality for permanent, automated storage of release notes
```

*Automated sync pipeline for data → game integration (pdoom1):*
```bash
# Automated data sync from pdoom-data to pdoom1
PDOOM_DATA_REPO="$(pwd)"
PDOOM1_REPO="${1:-../pdoom1}"
# Validate, rsync data, track commits, test integration
mkdir -p "$PDOOM1_REPO/shared/data/historical_timeline"
rsync -av --delete "$PDOOM_DATA_REPO/transformed/timeline_events/" "$PDOOM1_REPO/shared/data/historical_timeline/"
```

*Blog database schema example (pdoom-data):*
```sql
CREATE TABLE blog_entries (
    entry_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    title VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    slug VARCHAR(200) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    content TEXT NOT NULL,
    metadata JSONB,
    published_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    source_repo VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'pdoom1',
    sync_status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending'
);
```

*Session doc - summary of automation features:*
```
**Automated**: GitHub Actions trigger sync on pdoom-data commits
**Validated**: All data validated before sync
**Tested**: Integration tests run in pdoom1 PR
**Versioned**: Sync manifest tracks source commits
**Reversible**: Can rollback via git history
**Zero-sprawl**: Single source of truth (pdoom-data)
```

issues.title: Character Creation System (Point Allocation)
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement a character creation system where players allocate points to customize their starting position and abilities.

**Related to**: Risk System design (see `godot/docs/design/RISK_SYSTEM.md`)

---

## Feature Description

At game start, players can allocate points across:

### Starting Resources
- Extra money (runway buffer)
- Additional compute
- Starting reputation boost

### Starting Staff
- Pre-hired researchers (safety/capabilities mix)
- Starting manager

### Personality/Skills
- **Insight domains** (see #TBD - Insight System)
  - Political Insight (regulatory/public awareness visibility)
  - Financial Insight (funding/cash flow visibility)
  - Academic Insight (research integrity/capability overhang visibility)
  - Organizational Insight (insider threat visibility)
- Other personality traits TBD

### Default Class
- Pre-configured "default" option for players who don't want to customize
- Balanced starting position

---

## Design Notes

From design discussion (2025-12-25):
> "The game will have a 'build your character / starting equipment' section at the start where they can allocate points to give character eg extra money, staff, personality bonuses, etc, and default is just the default preconfig class."

This ties into:
- Insight system (skill-gated visibility into game mechanics)
- Upgrade rework (making upgrades more subtle)
- Player RPG element of the game

---

## UI Requirements
- Character creation screen before game start
- Point allocation interface
- Preview of starting position
- "Default" quick-start option
- Tooltip explanations for each option

---

## Dependencies
- Insight System (for skill allocation)
- Risk System (insight affects risk visibility)

---

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Character creation screen implemented
- [ ] Point allocation mechanic working
- [ ] At least 3 customizable dimensions
- [ ] Default class available
- [ ] Starting game state reflects allocations
- [ ] Integrates with insight system
